{{Quote|Hold your fire. There's no life forms. It must have short-circuited.|[[Gunnery captain/Legends|Gunnery captain]] [[Bolvan/Legends|Bolvan]], to [[Lieutenant]] [[Hija/Legends|Hija]]|<ref name="IV">{{Film|IV}}</ref>}}
A '''life form indicator''' ('''LFI'''), though typically grouped with [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]], was actually a sophisticated [[Computer/Legends|computer]] program designed to examine the information gathered by other sensors. The purpose of the life form indicator was to determine if a life-form was present and what kind of life-form it was. Information gathered by a [[Full-spectrum transceiver/Legends|full-spectrum transceiver]] on a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] might include data that a mobile heat-source was present, outputting at thirty [[Standard (temperature)|degrees Celsius]], and that the heat-source weighed eighty [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]]. The sensors would also determine that the ship's [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] was set at .96 Standard and the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] contained a large amount of [[Sulfur/Legends|sulfur]]. With these readings, a life form indicator program would determine from the data available that the heat-source was a [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]].<ref name="SWS" />

The quality of a starship's life form indicator was governed by the sensitivity of the vessel's onboard sensors, and the intelligence of the [[Computer/Legends|computer]] responsible for analyzing the data.<ref name="SWS" />
